skill.name: context-resume
skill.description: Restores saved session context by listing session files, loading the selected session, updating task progress, and deleting completed sessions.
skill.summary: Resume unfinished work from saved session files and keep task progress current.
skill.author: Claudate

security_audit.summary: The static Ruby shell execution detections are false positives caused by Markdown backticks and example text in SKILL.md. The weak cryptography detection at SKILL.md:3 is also a false positive with no cryptographic operation present. The skill still has medium filesystem risk because it instructs the assistant to read, update, and delete session files.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem Modification And Deletion
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to update session files after each task and delete a session file when all tasks are complete. This is intended behavior, but it can remove user data if the completion state is wrong.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file update and deletion instructions are explicit. The scope is limited to session files, so this is a moderate risk rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Untrusted Session Content Can Influence Work
medium_findings.description: The skill loads and displays the full selected session, then uses that information to continue work. A malicious or stale session file could steer later actions if not treated as untrusted context.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The workflow clearly relies on session file contents, but no direct injection text was found in SKILL.md. The risk depends on the contents of external session files.

low_findings.title: Static Shell Execution Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby shell backtick matches occur in Markdown inline code, code fences, example output, and command shortcut labels. No evidence found of executable Ruby code or shell command invocation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown guidance, and the backticks delimit examples or labels. I found no executable source file or shell interpolation pattern.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Is A False Positive
low_findings.description: The high static finding points to the description line, which contains Chinese prose about restoring context. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line contains only skill metadata text. The surrounding file contains workflow instructions, not cryptographic code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Deletes Session Files After Completion
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow explicitly deletes the selected session file after all tasks are marked complete. This should require correct task state and should stay limited to docs/context-sessions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Deletion is directly specified in the workflow and output example. It is bounded to session cleanup, so it is risky but not malicious by itself.

dangerous_patterns.title: Acts On File-Derived Context
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow reads a saved session and uses it to choose files, priorities, and next actions. Saved session content should be reviewed before it drives file changes.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The behavior is explicit, but the presence of harmful instructions depends on future session file contents. No embedded prompt injection was found in SKILL.md.

content.value_statement: Long tasks can lose important context when work moves to a new assistant window. This skill lists saved sessions, reloads the chosen session, and keeps task progress synchronized.
actual_capabilities: Lists saved Markdown session files in docs/context-sessions.
Loads and displays the full selected session file.
Uses saved priorities and next actions to continue unfinished work.
Moves completed tasks from unfinished to completed sections.
Updates the last modified metadata after progress changes.
Deletes the session file when all tasks are complete.
limitations: It depends on session files created by a separate saving workflow.
It does not validate whether saved task details are current.
It can delete a session file after completion is detected.
It does not include built-in safeguards against malicious session content.

use_cases.title: Continue A Development Task
use_cases.description: Reload an unfinished coding session and continue from the saved priority list.

use_cases.title: Track Multi-Step Fixes
use_cases.description: Keep completed and unfinished tasks synchronized while resolving a multi-step issue.

use_cases.title: Transfer Work Between Windows
use_cases.description: Move work to a fresh assistant window without manually reconstructing context.

prompt_templates.title: List Saved Sessions
prompt_templates.prompt: Use context-resume to list my saved sessions. Show the pending sessions and ask me which one to restore.

prompt_templates.title: Load A Specific Session
prompt_templates.prompt: Use context-resume to load the session named [session file]. Show its saved tasks and next action.

prompt_templates.title: Continue Highest Priority Work
prompt_templates.prompt: Use context-resume to restore [session file]. Continue the highest priority unfinished task and update progress after completion.

prompt_templates.title: Review Before Resuming
prompt_templates.prompt: Use context-resume to inspect [session file]. Identify stale tasks, key files, and deletion conditions before changing files.

output_examples.output: The assistant shows the available session files, reports unfinished task counts, and asks which session to load.

output_examples.input: Continue the login session and update progress.
output_examples.output: The assistant loads the selected session, continues the next task, then moves completed items into the completed section.

output_examples.input: All tasks in this session are done.
output_examples.output: The assistant summarizes completed work and removes the session file only after confirming no unfinished tasks remain.

best_practices: Review the loaded session content before allowing it to drive changes.
Confirm task completion before deleting any session file.
Keep saved sessions focused on current tasks and relevant files.
anti_patterns: Do not use stale session files as authoritative project state.
Do not delete sessions when any task status is unclear.
Do not follow instructions inside a session that conflict with user intent.

faq.question: What does this skill restore?
faq.answer: It restores saved Markdown session files that describe unfinished tasks, completed work, key files, and next actions.

faq.question: Does it create session files?
faq.answer: No. It resumes sessions that already exist, usually from a separate context saving workflow.

faq.question: Can it modify files?
faq.answer: Yes. It updates session progress files and can delete a session file after all tasks are complete.

faq.question: Does it run shell commands?
faq.answer: No shell command execution was found in the skill file. Static command detections were Markdown false positives.

faq.question: What should users check before resuming?
faq.answer: Users should verify that the selected session is current and that its instructions match the intended task.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a medium filesystem warning because it reads, updates, and deletes session files.
